to California State Standards.

actions.title:
Multi-Tiered System
of Support
actions.description:
Provide a rigorous, high-quality, standards-based instructional program in
English language arts, including English language development,
mathematics, science, history/social science, visual and performing arts,
and physical education aligned to the adopted California state standards.
As part of a comprehensive multi-tiered system of support, whole group
and targeted instruction for socioeconomically disadvantaged students,
foster youth, and English learners will be provided through tiered support to
address learning gaps. Supplemental resources and materials will be
utilized to meet the learning needs of our diverse learning population,
including print and electronic resources. While this serves students
districtwide, we expect the impact on academic measures of
socioeconomically disadvantaged students, foster youth, and English
learners to increase significantly more than the average of all other
students.
LREBG funds will be used to augment this action, $45,000

actions.title:
English Learner
Support
actions.description:
The instructional program for English learner (EL) students will be
designed and implemented to ensure they meet English language
development (ELD) standards for their respective grade levels. Students
will acquire full proficiency in English as rapidly and effectively as possible
to attain parity with native speakers within a reasonable time and achieve
the same rigorous, grade-level academic standards expected of all
students. This action is principally directed toward English learners to
provide targeted and designated ELD based on the language needs.
The district will provide professional development for teachers, focusing on
effective EL instruction techniques and integrating advanced educational
technologies that facilitate language learning.

actions.title:
Monitoring &
Assessing Student
Progress
actions.description:
The progress of English learner, foster youth, and socioeconomically
disadvantaged students' growth toward expected outcomes is monitored
and reviewed by the teacher. Staff will hold formal meetings to address
students not meeting expected outcomes, such as intervention or Student
Study Team (SST) meetings. Staff will assess, review, and report student
academic progress using various data sources, such as work samples,
observations, and assessments. Data gathered will be used by the
teacher, grade, school, and district to determine student progress.
State/local assessment data, student work samples, observations, and
presentations will be analyzed to determine if interventions are closing the
achievement gap. Action plans will be reviewed and revised as needed to
ensure students are making progress toward proficiency. Data systems will
be utilized at the district and site levels to assess the whole child and
determine targeted support for academic, social, emotional, and behavioral
intervention.

actions.title: Instructional Support
actions.description:
P r ovide school sites with highly qualified staff support who will assist in
implementing and monitoring district initiatives, curriculum development,
and the integration of technology into the classroom, with a focus on
unduplicated pupils. This encapsulates how highly qualified staff support
can be instrumental in improving educational outcomes. Providing our
schools with highly qualified staff support ensures that our educational
initiatives are implemented effectively, are inclusive, and are adaptable to
the needs of our English learners, foster youth, and economically
disadvantaged students, who face the greatest challenges. LREBG funds
will be used to augment this action, $800,996

actions.title:
Professional
Development
actions.description:
All certificated and classified staff will increase content and pedagogical
knowledge through collaboration and professional development. All
teachers and support staff will be trained to implement adopted programs
and strategies to provide a comprehensive, well-rounded, and rigorous
educational experience. Professional development will be of sufficient
intensity and duration to positively impact teacher and staff performance
and student achievement. These opportunities will be provided during staff
meetings, in-services, workshops, release days, etc. Enhancing
educational quality through professional development and collaboration
among all certificated and classified staff will directly impact unduplicated
pupils (those who are English learners, socioeconomically disadvantaged,
or foster youth).

actions.title:
English Learner
Support
actions.description:
Increase resources, interventions, and support for long-term English
learners with small-group instruction. Implement targeted small-group
instruction based on the student’s specific language proficiency and
academic needs. Provide professional development focused on
educational strategies such as scaffolding and culturally responsive
teaching. Support engagement with parents, providing them with the tools
and knowledge to support their child's language development at home.

actions.title: Intensive Supports
actions.description:
P rovide equitable access for students with exceptional needs to the full
array of services and tiered supports based on need and the student's
individual educational plan (IEP). This includes but is not limited to,
collaborative preschool options, a preschool through grade 6 learning
center model, and special day classes. Programs and services are aligned
to ensure free appropriate public education (FAPE).
The data clearly indicates that students with disabilities face significant
challenges within our educational system. This subgroup's performance in
ELA and math is notably lower than that of their general education peers,
with a substantial negative deviation from the standard. This action, while
applied districtwide, will be primarily focused at King Elementary in ELA.
The gaps identified underscore the critical need for action to provide these
students with equitable access to education, as mandated by the
Individuals with Disabilities Education Act (IDEA), which guarantees FAPE.
The action to provide equitable access to a continuum of services and
tiered supports is rooted in the recognition that students with disabilities
have unique learning needs that require specialized attention and
resources. The following points elaborate on why this action is necessary.
Individualized Educational Plans (IEP): Each student with a disability has
an IEP that outlines specific educational goals and the supports needed to
achieve them. Disparities in academic performance suggest a need for a
robust system that can fully implement these IEPs and ensure that
individual goals are met. Aligning services with the specific requirements of
each student's IEP ensures that interventions are personalized and directly
target the unique educational needs of students with disabilities. This
tailored approach is critical for enabling these students to access the full
curriculum and to participate fully in school activities, which is fundamental
to their academic and social integration.
Specialized Instructional Models:The action calls for collaborative
preschool options and learning center models from preschool through
grade 6, showing a commitment to early intervention, which is critical for
addressing learning difficulties as soon as they emerge.
Special day classes provide an environment where students can receive
intensive support in a setting tailored to their needs, which can be vital for
students where the general education classroom is not the least restrictive
environment suitable for them.
Aligned Programs and Services: Programs and services need to be
aligned across the district to ensure that students with disabilities can
access the general education curriculum to the greatest extent appropriate.
This alignment supports a seamless transition between different levels of
support as students progress through their education.
Focus on FAPE: This data-driven action affirms the district's obligation to
ensure that students with disabilities receive an education tailored to their
unique needs without cost to the family. This includes access to the
general education curriculum, specialized instructional strategies,
accommodations, modifications, and related services.
By taking this action, the district is adhering to legal requirements and
addressing the evident performance gaps highlighted by the data. It
reflects a commitment to an inclusive educational system where all
students, regardless of their abilities or disabilities, can thrive academically
and develop to their full potential. It is a proactive step toward ensuring that
every student is valued and supported in their individual learning
outcomes. This strategic initiative directly supports Goal 1 of our LCAP,
which aims to deliver every student a comprehensive, well-rounded, and
rigorous educational experience.

goals.description:
Provide a wide variety of parent/guardian and community involvement opportunities with open and
transparent communication to assure accurate, timely information.

actions.title:
Advisory
Opportunities & Input
actions.description:
Families will participate in decision-making regarding school and district
p r i o rities.
Advisory committees:
* School Site Councils (SSC)
* English Learner Advisory Committee (ELAC), as indicated on site
by-laws or will follow the SBE guidelines when incorporating in
SSC
* District English Learner Advisory Committee (DELAC)
* District Advisory Committee (DAC)
* Community Advisory Committee (Special Education)
* Other, as appropriate
Families of English learner, foster youth, and socioeconomically
disadvantaged students participate in the committees above. Providing
parents with decision making opportunities to actively inform and guide in
their students' educational programs is a crucial strategy for the Cypress
School District, aimed at enhancing parental involvement, especially for at-
risk student groups like Special Education students, foster youth,
socioeconomically disadvantaged families, and English learners.

actions.title: Communication
actions.description:
Provide timely communication, especially to families regarding their child's
educational program through various means, such as emails, phone calls,
and text messages. Maintain district/school websites and social media to
push out district/school information to families and the school community.
Utilize the parent portal for annual re-enrollment and at-a-glance student
information. Provide a variety of means for two-way communication
between staff, families, and the district, such as conferences, meetings,
emails, and phone calls.
Providing parents with multimodalities of communication enhances
parental involvement, especially for at-risk student groups like Special
Education students, foster youth, socioeconomically disadvantaged
families, and English learners. Robust communication through varied
means provides opportunities to employ one and two way communication
which
is a crucial strategy for the Cypress School District, aimed at enhancing
parental involvement, especially for at-risk student groups like Special
Education students, foster youth, socioeconomically disadvantaged
families, and English learners.

actions.title:
Community
Connection
actions.description:
Provide families, with a focus on foster youth, socioeconomically
disadvantaged families, and English learners, with the resources and
access to be active participants in their child’s educational programs
through a variety of involvement opportunities. The district and schools will
provide targeted outreach to parents/guardians of students in at-risk
subgroups to ensure parent participation is representative of the district
and school demographics, including families of foster youth,
socioeconomically disadvantaged students, and English learner students.
All sites make accommodations, as appropriate, for parents or guardians
with disabilities. Translation and/or interpretation will be provided as
appropriate. Providing parents with resources and access to actively
participate in their students' educational programs is a crucial strategy for
the Cypress School District, aimed at enhancing parental involvement,
especially for at-risk student groups like Special Education students, foster
youth, socioeconomically disadvantaged families, and English learners. To
support community connection, staff will participate in community
organizations and articulation opportunities to build relationships and share
the district's mission and vision.

actions.title:
Community Group
Collaboration
actions.description:
Cypress school district educational leaders will collaborate with local, state,
and federal groups. While the resources obtained as a result of these
partnerships may benefit our student population as a whole, this action is
principally directed toward our unduplicated pupils - English learner, foster
youth, and socioeconomically disadvantaged students. Staff participating
and collaborating with local, state and federal groups allows for family
perspective to be shared as well as enabling staff to identify resources and
supports aimed at enhancing parental involvement in the school and
broader community, with a lens for at-risk student groups like Special
Education students, foster youth, socioeconomically disadvantaged
families, and English learners.

goals.description: Provide an engaging, safe, and secure learning environment for all students.

actions.title: Student Services
actions.description:
Provide services and support by qualified staff to ensure continuity of
educational programs, support, and access to resources.
Emotional Support and Counseling: Mental health specialists provide
essential counseling services to students, helping them navigate the
complexities of school life and personal challenges. Their presence is
crucial for detecting and addressing emotional and psychological issues
that could impede a student’s ability to learn effectively.
Crisis Intervention: Mental health specialists are also key players in crisis
intervention, offering immediate support in times of need and helping to
manage situations that could escalate without professional intervention.
This support is essential for maintaining a safe school environment.
Staff Training and Support: Beyond student interaction, mental health
specialists equip school staff with the skills needed to recognize and
respond to mental health issues. This training enhances the overall
capacity of the school to address emotional and behavioral challenges
proactively.
Role of district Social-Emotional Learning (SEL) Teacher on Special
Assignment (TOSA)
* Curriculum Integration: Responsible for integrating social and
emotional learning into the school curriculum. SEL programs are
designed to teach students essential skills such as empathy, self-
regulation, and positive interaction, which are critical for both
academic and personal success.
* Professional Development: Provides ongoing professional
development to teachers, ensuring that SEL principles are
effectively incorporated into teaching strategies and classroom
management, which enhances the learning environment for
students.
* Program Development and Evaluation: Develops, implements, and
evaluates SEL programs across the district, ensuring these
initiatives align with educational goals and effectively support
student development.
Goal Impact
The inclusion of a mental health psychologist and a SEL TOSA directly
supports the district’s goal of creating a nurturing school environment by
addressing the holistic needs of the school community. These
professionals play a vital role in:
* Enhancing School Safety: By providing mental health and SEL
support, the district creates a safer school environment where
students are less likely to engage in conflict and more likely to
resolve disputes constructively.
* Promoting Inclusivity and Equity: These roles ensure that all
students, regardless of their personal circumstances or
backgrounds, receive the emotional and social support they need
to succeed.
* Building a Supportive Community: Their work fosters a sense of
community and belonging among students and staff, which is
essential for a positive school culture.
These mental health personnel are essential for achieving the overarching
goal of providing a safe, secure, and engaging learning environment. Their
expertise not only supports the emotional and psychological well-being of
students and staff but also strengthens the educational framework of the
district, making it a place where students can thrive academically and
personally. This strategic action underscores the district’s commitment to a
comprehensive education that values and nurtures the whole child. This
action is principally directed toward our unduplicated pupils - English
learner, foster youth, and socioeconomically disadvantaged students.
LREBG funds will be used to augment this action, $130,000.

actions.title:
Positive Supports &
Engagement
actions.description:
Implement a positive behavior and attendance system with Tier 1, 2, and 3
supports in the areas of social-emotional and behavior inclusive with a data
system to facilitate the management and monitoring of the positive
behavior system and social-emotional supports.
These tiered supports are particularly beneficial for our English learners,
socioeconomically disadvantaged, and foster youth students. They
address not only the academic challenges these students face but also the
social-emotional barriers to learning. By providing a structured yet flexible
support system, we aim to ensure that all students have the necessary
resources to develop positive behaviors, maintain regular attendance, and
ultimately achieve their full academic potential.
Tier 1: Provides universal strategies that promote positive behavior and
attendance across all students. This foundation level includes school-wide
initiatives such as setting clear behavioral expectations, recognizing
positive student behavior, and integrating social-emotional learning (SEL)
into the curriculum. These practices are aimed at creating a positive school
culture that encourages respectful and responsible behavior.
Tier 2: Targets support for groups of students who require more focused
interventions. These might include small group sessions that address
specific social skills, conflict resolution strategies, or additional SEL
lessons. The goal here is to preemptively address emerging behavioral
issues before they escalate, providing students with the tools they need to
navigate school challenges.
Tier 3: Offers intensive, individualized support for students who exhibit
significant behavioral challenges. This level includes personalized behavior
intervention plans, one-on-one counseling, and coordination with external
services when necessary. Such targeted interventions are critical for
students who struggle the most, ensuring they receive the comprehensive
support needed to succeed academically and socially.
To effectively manage and monitor our programs, we utilize a sophisticated
data system. This platform allows us to track student behavior and
attendance patterns, assess the effectiveness of interventions, and make
data-driven decisions. The system also facilitates communication among
teachers, mental health personnel, and administrators, enabling a
coordinated approach to supporting each student’s needs. This action is
principally directed toward our most at-risk subgroups, including foster
youth districtwide, two or more races at King Elementary, and students
with disabilities at Arnold Elementary.

actions.title: Attendance
actions.description:
Reduce barriers that hinder students from coming to school. Revise,
develop, and implement policies to increase access to and improve
participation in coordinated care in available school-based social-emotional
resources, positive behavior intervention systems (PBIS), and restorative
justice programs, including trauma-informed mental health services. This
action will be principally directed toward at-risk populations, including foster
youth districtwide, through the following:
Addressing Root Causes: Many absenteeism issues stem from underlying
social, emotional, or behavioral problems. By implementing policies that
provide comprehensive support, schools can directly address the root
causes of absenteeism. Trauma-informed mental health services are
particularly crucial for foster youth who might have experienced significant
trauma that affects their ability to attend or engage in school.
Creating Inclusive and Supportive Environments: School-based social-
emotional resources and positive behavior intervention systems help
create a more inclusive and supportive environment. These programs
teach coping and interpersonal skills, vital for students who may feel
alienated or overwhelmed by the school due to their circumstances.
Restorative Justice Programs: Restorative justice focuses on repairing
harm and restoring relationships rather than punitive measures. This
approach can significantly benefit students who might otherwise face
suspensions or expulsions, both disciplinary actions contributing to
absenteeism. It provides an alternative that keeps students in school and
engaged while addressing behavioral issues constructively.
Increasing Engagement and Participation: Schools can increase student
engagement and participation by implementing these systems. Students
are more likely to attend school if they feel safe, supported, and
understood. This is especially true for English learners and
socioeconomically disadvantaged students who might face additional
challenges that can alienate them from the school community.
Tailored Interventions: The action plan involves developing and revising
policies to ensure that interventions are tailored to meet the specific needs
of these vulnerable groups. By focusing on foster youth and extending
these practices to other high-risk groups, schools can ensure that
interventions are sensitive to all students' varied experiences and needs.
Integrated Support Systems: Coordinating care among different support
services within the school helps ensure that students receive seamless
support for their various needs. This integration is critical for effectiveness,
preventing students from falling through the cracks due to disjointed
service provision.
Outcome Expectation: Implementing these revised and developed policies
is expected to reduce chronic absenteeism among socioeconomically
disadvantaged students, English learners, and foster youth by providing
them with the necessary tools and support to overcome the barriers that
prevent regular school attendance. This will lead to improved academic
performance and better overall student well-being.

actions.title: Attendance
actions.description:
Reduce barriers that hinder students from coming to school. Revise,

resources, positive behavior intervention systems (PBIS), and restorative

Addressing Root Causes: Many absenteeism issues stem from underlying

particularly crucial for students who might have experienced significant

Creating Inclusive and Supportive Environments: School-based social-
emotional resources and positive behavior intervention systems help

Restorative Justice Programs: Restorative justice focuses on repairing

Increasing Engagement and Participation: Schools can increase student
engagement and participation by implementing these systems. Students
are more likely to attend school if they feel safe, supported, and
understood. This is especially important for students with disabilities and

Tailored Interventions: The action plan involves developing and revising

Elementary.
